title: Proper mutual tail calls on x86/arm32/riscv need an i128 tagged-value return ABI
goal: ESH-0102 made mutual tail calls proper R7RS tail calls by emitting LLVM `musttail` for a tail-position call to another function. `musttail` is now used on the AArch64 family (aarch64 / aarch64_be / arm64_32), where the backend lowers a struct-by-value return under musttail. It is NOT usable on x86_64 / i386 / arm(32) / riscv64: those backends fatally reject `musttail` for an AGGREGATE return ('failed to perform tail call elimination on a call site marked musttail'), and EVERY Eshkol function returns the by-value tagged value struct {i8,i8,i16,i32,i64}. Verified with `llc -mtriple=… -filetype=obj` on LLVM 21: scalar i64/i128 returns musttail fine on x86, but the struct return does not (even under tailcc). On those targets mutual tail calls therefore fall back to the old TCK_Tail hint (bounded stack), so a mutually tail-recursive state machine keeps a ~200-300k transition ceiling on x86. The root fix is to return the 16-byte tagged value as an i128 scalar (RAX:RDX / same registers, bit-compatible) so musttail lowers on x86 too — an ABI change touching every function signature, call site, and pack/unpack path.
acceptance: On x86_64 (and ideally arm32/riscv64), tests/stress/found/mutual_tail_1e7.esk prints 'OK ping' in O(1) stack under -r and AOT.
The P6b recursion_depth sweep mutual_tail2/mutual_tail3 5,000,000-hop cells PASS on x86_64.
No regression to the AArch64 path, SICP 88/88, AD oracle, or the tagged-value C-runtime interop (the i128 must remain bit-compatible with the 16-byte C struct).
campaign_payoff: R7RS proper tail calls should hold on every native target, not just AArch64; the canonical 'state machine as mutually tail-calling functions' idiom otherwise silently ceilings on x86.

verifications.evidence: ESH-0102 fix landed for AArch64 (musttail); x86/arm32/riscv64 aggregate-return musttail rejection isolated with llc on LLVM 21 and scoped to this ABI-change follow-up. See docs/design/MUTUAL_TAIL_CALLS.md.
